status: Fixed in foreign governance deferral lane
area: Git governance / Team Broker parallel lanes
finding: In a valid two-lane hotfile split, `node atm.mjs git commit --task TASK-RFT-0017 --auto-stage --defer-foreign-staged` still fail-closes when another active lane leaves a foreign `.atm/history/evidence/<other-task>.bundle-manifest.json` dirty. The bundle resolver already classifies the foreign file correctly, but it blocks the unrelated lane instead of offering a task-aware deferral or advisory path.
expectedBehavior: Team Broker style parallel lanes should be able to defer or ignore foreign same-repo generated evidence residue when the owning task is still active, as long as the file is outside the current task scope and is already attributed to a different task id. ATM must still fail closed for user-authored source overlap, but deterministic foreign bundle-manifest byproducts should have an explicit multi-lane policy instead of requiring manual lane-to-lane coordination every time.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by allowing foreign `.atm/history/evidence/<task>.bundle-manifest.json` files to be deferred under `--defer-foreign-staged`, excluding the deferred byproduct from same-attempt blocked residue, keeping it out of the current commit, and preserving it for the owning task. Closure packets, task events, task JSON, foreign source overlap, and unknown generated artifacts remain fail-closed. Verification: `node --strip-types tests/cli/git-commit-task-scoped-staging.test.ts` passes.
followUp: Keep fail-closed behavior for foreign source overlap and unknown generated artifacts; only known foreign bundle-manifest byproducts are deferred.
